title: Why so many miseries? | BG 8.15- 8.19 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: The topic is, why so many miseries in this world?
We have discussed this earlier also. On one side we see that God is our loving father.
He is very compassionate, he is very kind, he is very magnanimous.
On one side we see he is our father, he is loving, he is kind.
On the other side we see a lot of misery in this world. Am I right? Yes or no?
People dying of starvation and then floods, tsunamis, earthquakes, natural calamities, accidents,
innocent children getting crushed in accidents.
People not having enough food to eat.
Lot of anomalies in this world.
So sometimes you might wonder, where is God?
So today this discussion will be based on the teachings of His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada.
We will start with text 15 of the 8th chapter.
This discussion we have had many many times, but this is the verse today which I was referring to in many many of our previous sessions.
If you remember we had discussed,
I asked some of you what is the nature of this world and then we had three kinds of answers.
Some of us saying that it's a wonderful place, beautiful place, lovely place, beautiful flowers, wonderful landscape, sun rising in the morning, very scenic, very beautiful.
Some of us have had very good experiences in life so far, so we might opine that this world is a wonderful place.
It's a good place, great place.
And some of us also suggested that it's a good place.
Yes, even though there is misery, but yes, the world is wonderful.
Let's make the best use of whatever things, whatever unfolds in our life.
So that's one category of people who says yes, the world is great.
There's another category of people who unfortunately have not met with very pleasant experiences in life,
a lot of tragedies, may not be having good upbringing, financial problems, health problems, family problems.
And they might get frustrated and out of frustration say this world is bad.
There is a lot of misery in this.
They might even lose faith in the Lord and say there is no God here.
A person may be even pious, he may be God fearing, but still he may feel that his life is in shambles.
So there is another category who says this world is very miserable.
And what is the third category?
First category, it's a wonderful place.
Second category, it's a miserable place.
What is the third category of people?
One category says it's a wonderful place.
Another category says it's a miserable place.
Happiness and distress are two sides of the same coin.
Yes, we'll cover that as we move forward in the session.
So now let's understand what's the opinion of the Lord who has created this world, the Creator Himself.
You might have an opinion that the world is a wonderful place.
And suddenly in an accident, your near and dear one, you lose him or her.
Or some series of tragedies unfold in your life.

He was in the driver's seat.

Depends on the experiences what he goes through.
